key_insight: Hierarchical granular metamaterials combine lightweight architected grains, crystal-like packings, and programmable defects to boost energy absorption while lowering transmitted peak force.
engineering_relevance: This design offers a way to create low-density impact-mitigating materials with independently tunable mechanical dissipation and transport pathways.
method: Hollow elliptical inclusions inside architected grains
Crystal-inspired grain packing with functional gradients and defects
Multiscale impact testing plus nonlinear computational modeling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports in situ nanomechanical tests and multiscale impact experiments showing improved impact energy absorption per unit mass, reduced peak force transmission, and routing of deformation/charge pathways.
watch_item: Performance depends on precise control of grain geometry and contact topology, which may be difficult to manufacture consistently at scale.
